problem.summary: Visible VF, VSI, queue, vector, and mailbox identifiers can be reused across reset/reassignment; CapSched-H requires fresh epochs and monitor receipts so stale carriers cannot affect a new Domain.
unsafe_shortcuts: vf_id equality as VF identity freshness
ice_vf pointer reachability as Domain ownership
VSI index stability as MemoryView stability
queue id range validity as queue ownership
MSI-X vector remap as IRQ route authority
FDIR ctx_done validity as frozen completion authority
reset/rebuild replay as fresh Domain request

source_anchors.capsched_rule: Lookup is not current Domain binding authority.

source_anchors.existing_mechanism: VF id, VSI indices, FDIR state, queue bitmaps, num_msix, num_vf_qs, state bits, and opcode allowlist
source_anchors.capsched_rule: Visible fields need epoch-bearing carrier roots.

source_anchors.existing_mechanism: vf_id assigned sequentially and inserted into hash table
source_anchors.capsched_rule: New allocation must create a fresh VfEpochReceipt.

source_anchors.existing_mechanism: Default allowlist, num_msix, num_vf_qs, ctrl_vsi invalidation, FDIR init, mailbox init
source_anchors.capsched_rule: Initialization prepares Linux state; it does not mint queue, IRQ, DMA, or mailbox authority.

source_anchors.existing_mechanism: Driver caps negotiation, allowlist update, ACTIVE state
source_anchors.capsched_rule: ACTIVE must pair with fresh Domain/VF binding epoch.

source_anchors.existing_mechanism: Set DIS, trigger reset, disable queues, clear caps/allowlist, reset FDIR, release ctrl VSI, reconfig VSI, reset mailbox counters
source_anchors.capsched_rule: Reset start must embargo mailbox and invalidate old carriers; reopen requires fresh epochs.

source_anchors.existing_mechanism: Bulk reset of all VFs, per-VF rebuild, eswitch detach/attach
source_anchors.capsched_rule: Every affected VF binding and lower QueueLease must be epoch-bumped before attach/replay.

source_anchors.existing_mechanism: Disable queues, free IRQs, clear VPINT/GLINT/VPLAN mappings, release VSIs and VF entries
source_anchors.capsched_rule: Teardown must produce monitor revoke receipts before reuse or release.

source_anchors.existing_mechanism: Program VPINT, GLINT_VECT2FUNC, queue-base registers, free/reallocate vectors and rebuild queue mappings
source_anchors.capsched_rule: Vector and queue mapping changes require fresh IRQ route and QueueLease epochs.

source_anchors.existing_mechanism: Validate queue/vector ids, copy VF DMA ring bases, program queues, map interrupts, enable queues
source_anchors.capsched_rule: Requires fresh QueueLease, DMA MemoryView/IOMMU receipt, IRQ route receipt, and frozen queue config generation.

source_anchors.existing_mechanism: FDIR ctx_irq/ctx_done async completion and local init/exit cleanup
source_anchors.capsched_rule: Completion must carry current VF epoch, ctrl_vsi generation, rule generation, and frozen request carrier.

source_anchors.existing_mechanism: vf_id lookup, cfg_lock, DIS check, virtchnl validation, opcode allowlist, operation dispatch
source_anchors.capsched_rule: Mailbox ingress must freeze a VFRequestCarrier with current vf_epoch and mailbox_generation.

safe_handoff_sequence: clear or epoch-tag async FDIR context
bind new Domain and VSI generation
derive fresh QueueLease and route receipts
authorize service replay under fresh carrier
design_constraints: No old epoch effect may survive handoff into a new Domain.
Visible identifiers can be reused only after authority epochs change.
Linux reset cleanup is substrate, not the security root.
Monitor receipts are required for DMA, IRQ, queue, and VF epoch authority.
Cluster migration must preserve the same revoke/bump/reopen sequence.
